notes: Do not submit these declarations until the actual binary is exercised in Light and Dark appearances.
Apple's Accessibility Nutrition Labels should be based on common tasks, not on competitor labels.
Larger Text is listed by Apple as not supported for Mac product-page labels.

currentEvidence: Core app surfaces are mostly AppKit menus, alerts, and windows.
No custom dark-mode-only rendering has been verified yet.
remainingChecks: Launch the next build in Light and Dark appearances.
Check first launch, menu bar menu, purchase/restore, send window, QR fallback, Share extension, and error alerts.

currentEvidence: Native controls handle most core UI contrast.
Generated App Store screenshots use dark text on pale backgrounds and should be checked at thumbnail scale.
remainingChecks: Check custom screenshot assets and generated imagery.
Check disabled menu items, status text, QR fallback, and purchase dialogs.

currentEvidence: Menu items and transfer states use text labels in addition to colors.
Generated screenshots still use green arrows and status dots, but core meaning is also conveyed by text and layout.
remainingChecks: Verify errors, progress, purchase state, and transfer completion are not communicated only by color.

currentEvidence: Some custom views set accessibilityDescription to nil for icons.
Focus order and labels have not been exercised.
remainingChecks: Run VoiceOver through all common tasks before considering a claim.

currentEvidence: Voice Control operation has not been exercised.
remainingChecks: Check menu commands, device selection, purchase flow, and Share extension operation.

currentEvidence: Apple's Accessibility Nutrition Labels overview says Larger Text is not supported on Mac.
No motion-heavy UI was identified in static review, but system reduced-motion behavior has not been verified.
remainingChecks: Verify any progress, QR, notification, or purchase animations respect system settings before claiming.

currentEvidence: The app has no video or audio-only content as part of common tasks.
The app has no video content as part of common tasks.
